title: RETURNING AN ITEM TO THE STORE
conversation: Paul: Hi, I bought this camera here yesterday, and it's not working. Tim: Did you charge the battery and put it in correctly? Paul: Yes. I followed the directions to the letter. Tim: Do you mind if I take a crack at it? Paul: Be my guest. Tim: I usually have the magic touch . . . Hmmm, you're right. It doesn't work. Would you like a replacement? Paul: No, this is the second one I've had to return. I think I'll steer clear of this model. Tim: I hear what you're saying. It's frustrating when you get one bad unit, let alone, two! Paul: Yeah, it's a bummer! I'd like my money back. Tim: If you give me your receipt, I'll issue a credit to your credit card. But you might also try a different model or brand. Paul: Maybe I will try a different camera. Tim: We've got an excellent Canon camera that's the same price as this one. It would be an even exchange. Paul: Okay, I'll take it. I hope this one works out. Tim: If you're not happy with it, don't hesitate to bring it back.

idioms.meaning_en: following instructions or rules exactly and precisely
idioms.meaning_vi: làm theo đúng từng li từng tí, tuân thủ nghiêm ngặt
idioms.example: He followed the recipe to the letter, so the cake turned out perfectly.
idioms.context: Yes. I followed the directions to the letter.

idioms.meaning_en: to try to do something, especially something you have not done before
idioms.example: I've never fixed a car before, but I'll take a crack at it.
idioms.context: Do you mind if I take a crack at it?

idioms.meaning_en: a polite way of telling someone to go ahead and do something
idioms.meaning_vi: cứ tự nhiên, xin cứ việc
idioms.example: If you want to use my phone, be my guest.

idioms.meaning_en: to have a special ability to make something successful or work well
idioms.meaning_vi: có bàn tay phù thủy, có biệt tài làm mọi thứ thành công
idioms.example: Everything she plants grows well; she really has the magic touch in the garden.
idioms.context: I usually have the magic touch . . .

idioms.meaning_en: to avoid someone or something because they are unpleasant or dangerous
idioms.meaning_vi: tránh xa cái gì hoặc ai đó
idioms.example: You should steer clear of that neighborhood at night.
idioms.context: I think I'll steer clear of this model.

idioms.meaning_en: to understand or empathize with someone's point of view
idioms.meaning_vi: Tôi hiểu những gì bạn đang nói/chia sẻ
idioms.example: I hear what you're saying about the price, but the quality is much better.

idioms.meaning_en: used to emphasize that something is even more unlikely or impossible than the first thing mentioned
idioms.example: I can't afford a new bike, let alone a new car.
idioms.context: It's frustrating when you get one bad unit, let alone, two!

idioms.meaning_en: a situation that is disappointing, annoying, or unpleasant
idioms.meaning_vi: một sự thất vọng, điều đáng tiếc, điều xui xẻo
idioms.example: It's a real bummer that the concert was canceled because of rain.

idioms.meaning_en: to refund money back to a customer's account
idioms.meaning_vi: hoàn tiền, cấp một khoản tín dụng hoàn trả
idioms.example: The store will issue a credit to your account within five business days.
idioms.context: If you give me your receipt, I'll issue a credit to your credit card.

idioms.meaning_en: trading one item for another of equal value
idioms.meaning_vi: đổi ngang giá, đổi đồng giá
idioms.example: Since the two shirts cost the same, it was an even exchange.
idioms.context: It would be an even exchange.

idioms.meaning_en: to happen or develop in a satisfactory or successful way
idioms.meaning_vi: diễn ra tốt đẹp, có kết quả tốt
idioms.example: I hope your new job works out for you.
idioms.context: I hope this one works out.

idioms.meaning_en: do not wait or be worried about doing something; feel free to do it
idioms.example: If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to ask.
idioms.context: If you're not happy with it, don't hesitate to bring it back.
